description_full:
The bitwise AND of an array nums is the bitwise AND of all integers in nums.


	For example, for nums = [1, 5, 3], the bitwise AND is equal to 1 & 5 & 3 = 1.
	Also, for nums = [7], the bitwise AND is 7.


You are given an array of positive integers candidates. Compute the bitwise AND for all possible combinations of elements in the candidates array.

Return the size of the largest combination of candidates with a bitwise AND greater than 0.

 
Example 1:


Input: candidates = [16,17,71,62,12,24,14]
Output: 4
Explanation: The combination [16,17,62,24] has a bitwise AND of 16 & 17 & 62 & 24 = 16 > 0.
The size of the combination is 4.
It can be shown that no combination with a size greater than 4 has a bitwise AND greater than 0.
Note that more than one combination may have the largest size.
For example, the combination [62,12,24,14] has a bitwise AND of 62 & 12 & 24 & 14 = 8 > 0.


Example 2:


Input: candidates = [8,8]
Output: 2
Explanation: The largest combination [8,8] has a bitwise AND of 8 & 8 = 8 > 0.
The size of the combination is 2, so we return 2.


 
Constraints:


	1 <= candidates.length <= 105
	1 <= candidates[i] <= 107

The size of the combination is 4.

subtasks.intent: Iterate through all possible bit positions and for each position, count how many numbers in the input array have that bit set. The maximum count found across all bit positions will be the result.
